title: ToolLibrary Object
description: ToolLibrary represents a collection of Tool objects.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Inserts a Tool at the end of the ToolLibrary.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createFromJson
methods.description: Creates a ToolLibrary by given JSON-string. Raises an error if the given JSON is invalid.

methods.name: createQuery
methods.description: Creates a new ToolQuery that is used to query the library for tools matching the query.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Get Tool by index in ToolLibrary.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Remove Tool by index from ToolLibrary.

methods.name: toJson
methods.description: Generate and return JSON string that contains all tools of that list.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of tools in the ToolLibrary.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Hole and Pocket Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates three different methods for feature recognition: one for holes and two for pockets.The script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to demonstrate the three methods. After the features are recognised they are coloured and milling and drilling operations are created for each feature.RecognizedHoleGroup returns a list of BRepFaces that can be used as selections for the drilling operation. RecognizedPocket also return some BRepFaces but it needs additional processing before the output can be used for creating machining operations.The sample script demonstrates a couple of different methods, including finding the pocket BRepFaces and creating sketches from the recognized pockets.This script works only if the Manufacturing Extension is active.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

related_samples.name: Turning Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Turning Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by opening a simple component which is then used to describe a basic turning workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

add.title: ToolLibrary.add Method
add.description: Inserts a Tool at the end of the ToolLibrary.
add.return_description: Returns true for successful insertion, false otherwise

parameters.name: tool
parameters.description: The Tool that should be added.

classType.title: ToolLibrary.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createFromJson.title: ToolLibrary.createFromJson Method
createFromJson.description: Creates a ToolLibrary by given JSON-string. Raises an error if the given JSON is invalid.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: The JSON contains all tools that should be added to the new ToolLibrary

createQuery.title: ToolLibrary.createQuery Method
createQuery.description: Creates a new ToolQuery that is used to query the library for tools matching the query.
createQuery.return_description: Returns a new ToolQuery. The query is predefined by given parameter.

related_samples.name: Hole and Pocket Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates three different methods for feature recognition: one for holes and two for pockets.The script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to demonstrate the three methods. After the features are recognised they are coloured and milling and drilling operations are created for each feature.RecognizedHoleGroup returns a list of BRepFaces that can be used as selections for the drilling operation. RecognizedPocket also return some BRepFaces but it needs additional processing before the output can be used for creating machining operations.The sample script demonstrates a couple of different methods, including finding the pocket BRepFaces and creating sketches from the recognized pockets.This script works only if the Manufacturing Extension is active.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

item.title: ToolLibrary.item Method
item.description: Get Tool by index in ToolLibrary.
item.return_description: Returns the Tool in the ToolLibrary by given index.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Index of the Tool in the ToolLibrary.

remove.title: ToolLibrary.remove Method
remove.description: Remove Tool by index from ToolLibrary.
remove.return_description: Returns true for successful deletion, false otherwise

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Index of the Tool in the ToolLibrary that should be removed.

toJson.title: ToolLibrary.toJson Method
toJson.description: Generate and return JSON string that contains all tools of that list.
toJson.return_description: Returns JSON string that contains all tools of that list.

count.title: ToolLibrary.count Property
count.description: The number of tools in the ToolLibrary.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = toolLibrary_var.count

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
